PRODUCED BY THE T(D,N) REACTION ON A 2.5MG/CM*2
THICK TI-T TARGET WITH 2 MEV DEUTERONS AND A
COLLIMATOR ANGLE OF 97 DEG TO THE FORWARD DIRECTION
FOR 12.4 MEV NEUTRONS THE COLLIMATOR WAS TURNED
TARGET THICKNESS WAS 12.5 MG/CM*2 GIVING A NEUTRON
FOR 6 TO 9MEV NEUTRONS THE D(D,N) REACTION
AND A DEUTERIUM GAS CELL WERE USED. COLLIMATOR AT
5 DEGREES AND 2MM APERTURE. THE 3.2 MG/CM*2 MO
WINDOW TO THE GAS CELL ACCOUTED FOR 140 TO 180KEV
LOSS IN NEUTRON ENERGY. NEUTRON ENERGY SPREAD OF
.THE NUMBER OF 20 MICRO-SEC GATES AFTER A NEUTRON
PULSE CONTAINING 2 OR 3 PULSES FROM NEUTRON
NEUTRONS DETECTED IN LARGE LOADED LIQ SCIN
NEUTRON FLUX ATTENUATION IN SAMPLE AND THE U-238
PRESENCE OF LOWER ENERGY NEUTRONS IN INCIDENT BEAM
MULTIPLE EVENTS IN BEAM BURST, DOUBLE FISSIONS
THE EFFECT OF NEUTRON ENERGY SPREAD ON STANDARD
LOSS OF SECONDARY NEUTRONS IN THE SAMPLE.
NON-UNIFORM ENERGY RESPONSE OF THE LIQUID SCINT.
MULTIPLE EVENTS IN BEAM BURST, NEUTRON PULSES
CHANGE IN COUNTER EFFICIENCY WITH SAMPLE (LT 0.2PC)
PRODUCTION OF (N,2N) EVENTS IN STEEL TUBES.
LOSS OF NEUTRONS THROUGH AXIAL HOLE.
GIVEN IS THE WEIGHTED MEAN OF MEASUREMENTS ON
.POWDER CLAD IN ALUMINIUM AND UNCLAD INGOT.
